name: Basilisk
primary_sources: Naturalis Historia by Pliny the Elder
Schedula diversarum artium by Theophilus Presbyter

visual_attributes.morphology: A small snake-like creature with a crown-shaped crest on its head.
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Serpentine body with a prominent head crest

domain_specific_visual.MYTHOLOGICAL_ICONOGRAPHY: Crown-shaped crest symbolizes its status as 'king of serpents'

functional_attributes.primary_function: Causes death with its lethal gaze and venomous presence

domain_specific_functional.ALCHEMICAL_ROLE: Used in alchemical recipes for transformation

relationships.description: The weasel's odor is fatal to the basilisk.

relationships.target: Egg of a serpent or toad
relationships.description: Believed to be hatched by a cockerel from such an egg.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Primarily Europe, with influences from Asiatic and African snake accounts
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Symbolizes lethal power and is a guardian of treasures
primary_sources: Naturalis Historia by Pliny the Elder

contrastive_constraints: NOT a purely fictional creature without mythological roots
